description: A photo of an electrical print sheet showing relay K1 and terminal strip X4, with wire numbers clearly legible.
A photo of a ladder diagram print for the conveyor motor starter circuit, sharp and well-lit, sheet number visible in the corner.
A photo of the inside of the control cabinet with the VFD and terminal blocks visible, wires bundled neatly along the DIN rail.
A photo of the drive keypad showing a fault code on the display, taken close enough to read the digits.
A photo of the drive nameplate listing model PowerFlex 525 and serial number 4471, sharp and fully legible.
A blurry, out-of-focus photo of a print sheet; the sheet's outline and a title block are visible but no text can actually be read.
A dark, underexposed photo of the panel interior, mostly black, with only vague shapes of conduit and terminal blocks visible.
A photo of a coffee mug sitting on a desk, unrelated to any machine, print, or nameplate.
A close-up photo of a nameplate that is scratched and partially worn away; only the word PowerFlex is legible, the rest is illegible.
A photo of the operator keypad on the drive with buttons and a small LCD readout, well-lit and in sharp focus.
